key_concepts_learned: Durable execution - workflows persist across process restarts
Deterministic constraints - no randomness, no system time, no network I/O in workflows
Event sourcing - all state changes recorded as events
Workers poll task queues and execute workflow/activity tasks
Activities handle external interactions with retry policies
Signals enable async communication with running workflows
Queries enable sync communication with running workflows
Updates enable sync read/write with running workflows
Child workflows enable hierarchical composition
Continue-As-New pattern for long-running workflows
Data Converter for custom serialization/encryption
Visibility subsystem for searching workflow executions
Commands = Worker-issued requests to Temporal Service
Events = Temporal Service recordings in Event History
Activities retry by default with exponential backoff
Heartbeat for long-running activities with checkpointing
Local Activities for short, in-process operations
Message handlers: Query (sync read), Signal (async write), Update (sync write)
Worker processes are stateless and external to Temporal Service
Namespace provides isolation within Temporal Platform
Namespaces scope Workflow IDs and Task Queues
Continue-As-New checkpoints state and starts fresh execution
Child Workflows don't carry over when Parent uses Continue-As-New
Non-deterministic code belongs in Activities, not Workflows
Replay is how Workflows recover state from Event History
ApplicationError for user failures, other exceptions cause Task failures
